req.title: System-Admin creates training-course
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to create a training-course through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path creation by an authenticated admin (EP – valid partition), (2) missing mandatory fields returning 400 (NEG), (3) title at exact max-length boundary (BVA), (4) unauthenticated request returning 401 (NEG/DT), (5) forbidden role returning 403 (DT), and (6) state transition from non-existent to created + audit record written (STC). Six tests satisfy 'at least one positive, one negative, one boundary' with full heuristic coverage.

tests.name: test_admin_creates_training_course_valid_payload
preconditions: Payload contains all mandatory fields with valid values
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <admin_token fixture>
POST /api/training-courses with full valid payload
Capture response
expected: Response body contains an entity id
An audit record exists for the created course id
trace: R-WEB-045 – expected outcomes: training-course is created; audit record written; response 200/201 with entity id

tests.name: test_create_training_course_missing_mandatory_title_returns_400
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <admin_token fixture>
POST /api/training-courses with payload missing 'title'
Capture response
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing 'title'
trace: R-WEB-045 – error pathways: 400 on validation error

tests.name: test_create_training_course_title_at_max_boundary_length
preconditions: API spec defines max title length as 255 characters
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <admin_token fixture>
Build a title string of exactly 255 characters
POST /api/training-courses with that title
Capture response
expected: No truncation of the title in the returned resource
trace: R-WEB-045 – mandatory fields must be validated; BVA on maximum allowed title length (255 chars)
tests.executable:
def test_create_training_course_title_at_max_boundary_length(admin_token, api_client):
    max_title = "A" * 255
    payload = {
        "title": max_title,
        "description": "BVA boundary test",
        "duration_minutes": 45,
        "is_mandatory": False
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/api/training-courses",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body and body["id"]
    assert body.get("title") == max_title

tests.name: test_create_training_course_title_exceeds_max_boundary_returns_400
preconditions: API spec defines max title length as 255 characters
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <admin_token fixture>
Build a title string of 256 characters (max + 1)
POST /api/training-courses with that title
Capture response
expected: Response body references title length validation failure
trace: R-WEB-045 – mandatory fields must be validated; BVA on one-above maximum title length (256 chars)

tests.name: test_create_training_course_unauthenticated_returns_401
actions: POST /api/training-courses with valid payload but no Authorization header
Capture response
expected: Response body indicates authentication is required

tests.name: test_create_training_course_non_admin_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not admin)
actions: Obtain non-admin auth token via <employee_token fixture>
POST /api/training-courses with valid payload
Capture response
trace: R-WEB-045 – error pathways: 403 if role forbidden; actors: system-admin only

tests.name: test_create_training_course_state_transitions_to_active_and_audit_logged
preconditions: No prior course with the same title exists (idempotency via unique title helper)
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <admin_token fixture>
POST /api/training-courses to transition state from NON_EXISTENT to CREATED
GET /api/training-courses/{id} to verify state is active
Check audit log for CREATE transition record
expected: POST returns 200 or 201 with entity id
GET confirms course status is 'active' or equivalent initial state
Audit record exists with action CREATE and correct entity id
trace: R-WEB-045 – expected outcomes: training-course is created; audit record written; STC NON_EXISTENT -> CREATED
tests.executable:
def test_create_training_course_state_transitions_to_active_and_audit_logged(
    admin_token, api_client, unique_title, audit_record_exists
):
    payload = {
        "title": unique_title("STC Course"),
        "description": "State transition coverage test",
        "duration_minutes": 90,
        "is_mandatory": True
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    create_resp = api_client.post("/api/training-courses", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert create_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    course_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/api/training-courses/{course_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json().get("status") in ("active", "published", "enabled")
    assert audit_record_exists(entity_id=course_id, action="CREATE", entity_type="training-course")
